---
title: "Construction Issues"
---

::: callout-note
**Coming in a future release.** This chapter documents a planned addition to the AutoDeskR package. The API described here exists in APS but R wrapper functions are not yet available. Track progress at [github.com/paulgovan/AutoDeskR](https://github.com/paulgovan/AutoDeskR).
:::

# Construction Issues

The [Construction Issues API](https://aps.autodesk.com/en/docs/acc/v1/overview/field-guide/issues/) provides programmatic access to the issue tracking system in AutoDesk Construction Cloud (ACC). Issues are used by field teams to log defects, RFIs, observations, and punch-list items against a project.

From R, this API enables you to pull all open issues into a data frame, build quality dashboards, track resolution rates over time, or trigger alerts when critical issues are created.

## Why It Matters

Construction projects generate hundreds to thousands of issues. R's data manipulation and visualisation tools — dplyr, ggplot2, Shiny — are well suited to analysing this data, but only if it can be accessed programmatically. The Construction Issues API provides that access.

Example analyses:
- Distribution of open vs. closed issues by trade
- Average time-to-resolution by issue type
- Issue heat map by location (floor / zone)
- Automated weekly issue summary emailed via `blastula`

## Planned Functions

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `listIssues(token, project_id, ...)` | List all issues in a project (paginated) |
| `getIssue(token, project_id, issue_id)` | Retrieve a single issue with full details |
| `createIssue(token, project_id, ...)` | Create a new issue |
| `updateIssue(token, project_id, issue_id, ...)` | Update an issue's status, assignee, or due date |
| `listIssueTypes(token, project_id)` | List available issue types and subtypes |
| `listIssueCustomAttributes(token, project_id)` | List custom attribute definitions |

## Planned Workflow

```{r}
#| eval: false
# (Functions not yet implemented — illustrative only)
library(AutoDeskR)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

token      <- getToken(id, secret, scope = "data:read")
project_id <- "b.abc12345-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

# Pull all issues
issues_raw <- listIssues(token, project_id = project_id, limit = 200)
issues_df  <- issues_raw$content$results

# Summarise by status
issues_df |>
  count(status) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(status, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#6aaa2a") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Issues by Status", x = NULL, y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Issue Object Structure

Each issue returned by the API has the following key fields:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `id` | Unique issue identifier |
| `title` | Short description |
| `status` | `open`, `in_review`, `closed` |
| `issueType` | Type (e.g. `"Defect"`, `"RFI"`) |
| `assignedTo` | Assignee user ID |
| `dueDate` | ISO 8601 date string |
| `createdAt` | Creation timestamp |
| `updatedAt` | Last update timestamp |
| `locationDescription` | Free-text location (e.g. `"Level 3, Grid C4"`) |
